author: śrīla locana⋅dāsa ṭhākura
verses:
parama karuṇa, pa̐hu duijana,
nitāi gaura⋅candra
saba avatāra⋅, sāra śiromaṇi,
kevala ānanda⋅kanda

bhaja bhaja bhāi, caitanya⋅nitāi,
sudṛḍha viśvāsa kari’
viṣaya chāḍiyā, se⋅rase majiyā,
mukhe bala hari hari

dekha ore bhāi, tri-bhuvane nāi,
emana dayāla dātā
paśu pākhī jhure, pāṣāṇa vidare,
śuni’ jā̐’ra guṇa-gāthā

saṁsāre majiyā, rahile paḍiyā,
se⋅pade nahila āśa
āpana karama, bhuñjāya śamana,
kahaye locana⋅dāsa
en_translation:
Supremely merciful are the two Lords, Nitāi and Gaura⋅candra. They are the crest-jewel and the essence of all incarnations—the only source of joy.

Worship them, O brother! Worship Caitanya and Nitāi with firm faith. Giving up material attachments and absorbed in that divine mellow, continuously chant "Hari! Hari!"

See, O brother! In the three worlds there are no givers so merciful. Animals and birds weep, and even stones melt upon hearing their glorious qualities.

Remaining fallen and drowning in the material world, no desire arose in me for their lotus feet. One's own karma makes one experience the consequences of death—so says Locana dāsa.
